resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def lastSubstring(self, s: str) -> str:
        i = len(s) - 1
        while i > 0:
            if s[i] > s[i - 1]:
                return s[i - 1:]
            i -= 1
        return s
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Iterates through the string from the end, comparing characters. Returns the substring starting from the first character smaller than the one before it. If no such character is found, returns the entire string.  This approach efficiently finds the lexicographically largest substring in linear time.
